channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The New Mexico judge who presided over Alec Baldwin's trial is set to retire
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A New Mexico state district judge who presided over the widely publicized trial of actor Alec Baldwin for a fatal movie set shooting is getting ready to retire from the bench. Court administrators announced Mary Marlowe Sommer’s upcoming retirement on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspects in Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths plead not guilty
item.description: Jordan Willis and Ivory J. Carson pleaded not guilty during their arraignment in connection with the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, KRON4

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump targets security clearances of law firm entangled in 2016 Russia investigation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday he was moving to suspend the security clearances of attorneys at a prominent law firm linked to since-discredited opposition research during the 2016 presidential campaign into any ties between the Republican candidate and Russia.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that killed seven people when it plummeted into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January and likely had not been functioning for several years, the National Transportation Safety Board said in a preliminary rep
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Black box of ambulance jet that crashed in Philly wasn't recording audio, likely hadn't worked for years: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder on a plane that crash-landed in Philadelphia was not working and apparently did not work for several years, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico adult dies with measles, though cause of death isn't yet confirmed
item.description: An adult who was infected with measles has died in New Mexico, state health officials announced Thursday, though the virus has not been confirmed as the cause. The person who died was unvaccinated and did not seek medical care, a state health department spokesperson said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's move to restore old Army base names highlights Nebraska man's heroics at 18 in World War I
item.description: For more than a century, Fort Benning’s name honored a Confederate general who supported slavery. The military changed the name of the Army base in Georgia two years ago, but now the Trump administration is set on restoring the familiar one — this time for a different Benning.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As DOGE slashes spending, it's missing millions of dollars hidden in plain sight
item.description: As DOGE looks for efficiencies, it may need to start with the government’s own money sitting unclaimed in many states, held by the government.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: A 40-day Target boycott began this week. What to know about the protest and its potential impact
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A 40-day boycott of Target that calls for supporters to give up shopping at the company's stores during the Lenten period kicked off this week, to protest the discount retailer's decision to end some of its diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump slashed teacher training, citing DEI. Educators say the grants fought staff shortages
item.description: Trump administration cuts to teacher training grants are putting a strain on rural school systems, which have relied on the money to help address teacher shortages.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California's Gavin Newsom opposes trans athletes in women's sports, splitting with progressives
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has spoken out against allowing transgender women and girls to compete in female sports.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: Authorities in Alaska say they will attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen And Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing Trump's threats, Columbia investigates students critical of Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University senior Maryam Alwan was visiting family in Jordan over winter break when she received an email from the school accusing her of discriminatory harassment. Her supposed top offense: writing an op-ed in the student newspaper calling for divestment from Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill to pay troops during shutdown filed as funding deadline looms
item.description: Rep. Jen Kiggans (R-Va.) reintroduced a bill Thursday to ensure that members of the military get paid during a government shutdown, a move that comes as a March 14 deadline ticks closer without a deal to extend funding.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WGN 9

item.title: Louisiana and Arkansas look to nitrogen executions
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Hours after the nation’s first execution by nitrogen gas in January last year, Alabama's attorney general urged other states to also develop it as a method for carrying out death sentences. Now, some states are following suit.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, KIM CHANDLER and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bryan Kohberger's defense claims he has autism in bid to avoid firing squad
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's autism diagnosis should take the death penalty off the table if he is convicted of the Idaho student murders, defense lawyer argues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants clone Border Patrol vehicles in bid to bypass Trump's crackdown
item.description: Some human smugglers have turned to cloning Border Patrol vehicles to bypass President Trump's crackdown on illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denver mayor grilled over area’s Tren de Aragua problem as GOP lawmaker says policies to blame
item.description: Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston is taking heat from state lawmakers over "weak immigration laws" and faced Congress over sanctuary status this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Avalanche buries 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska, officials say
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of dozens of feet of snow, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday. If the deaths are confirmed, it would be the deadliest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian refugee in Las Vegas rebuilds life with charity's help
item.description: "I am so grateful for Lighthouse Charities; they help a lot for me with my first car, with my apartment, with my food.
item.dc:creator: Mary Jane Belleza, NewsNation

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US travel advisory for Turks and Caicos Islands updated, year after Americans arrested
item.description: The updated advisory comes nearly a year after several Americans were arrested on ammunition charges in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Fiancée of Faster Pussycat singer dies after going overboard on '80s-themed cruise, family says
item.description: Kimberly Burch, the fiancée of an '80s rock star, has died after going overboard an '80s-themed cruise, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KRON4

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
